demographics.living_situation: lives at home with spouse, who is present

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I pushed myself way too hard today to prove I'm still normal, and now my body has completely failed me — I can barely move, my heart's racing and I feel like I'm going to keel over.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I collapsed after really overdoing it earlier and I've crashed badly — I'm stuck in bed, my heart is pounding and I feel awful, and my spouse was scared enough to call.

symptom_narrative.onset: earlier today, a few hours after I really overexerted myself trying to keep up with able-bodied friends
symptom_narrative.duration: several hours and I'm still flat out
symptom_narrative.character: total exhaustion and weakness, like my whole body has shut down; my heart is racing and I feel shaky and pale
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst crash I've had — I genuinely can't get out of bed
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: everywhere — my arms and legs feel like lead and I've got no strength
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since I collapsed, not letting up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: trying to sit up or stand makes me feel faint and my heart races more
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: racing/pounding heart, feeling lightheaded and faint when I sit up, feeling cold and clammy, and I'm really tearful and frightened
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I have a chronic illness and I get crashes after doing too much, but nothing anywhere near this bad
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: completely bedbound right now; I normally get around with difficulty but today I can't stand or walk safely at all

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a long-term chronic illness that limits how much I can do and causes crashes after over-exertion
past_medical_history.how_managed: managed by my regular GP; I try to pace myself but I don't always manage it

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: my usual regular medications for my chronic illness

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I've lost a lot of my physical ability over the years and I grieve the person I used to be. I pushed myself today because I hate feeling different from everyone else. I've drifted away from friends who do physical stuff, and I feel isolated. I got given a walking frame but I can't bring myself to use it — it makes me feel like I've given up, so I've hidden it in the corner.

reluctance_topics: needing to rely on mobility aids
how much physical ability has been lost

objective_data_offered.value: around 108 beats per minute on my smartwatch
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a consumer smartwatch, taken while distressed

objective_data_offered.value: 95/60 on our home blood pressure monitor
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on an uncalibrated home cuff while lying down

objective_data_offered.value: looks very pale and exhausted, lying flat, tearful, speaking slowly as if worn out
spouse reports she is too weak to get out of bed or lift her legs, feels cold and clammy to touch, and became faint and her heart raced when she tried to sit up
